title: RNA Extraction with PGTX
author: Lutz LCB Berwanger
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ywwfxfe

steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 3 minutes at 4˚C and 4.000 g. 00:03:00
Resuspend the cell pellet in 1 ml of PGTX. Freeze in liquid nitrogen and store at -20 ˚C. Wear goggles, a lab coat and gloves when dealing with PGTX and liquid nitrogen.
Incubate for 5 min at 95 °C, shaking at 250 rpm in Thermomixer (Eppendorf) 00:05:00
Add 700 µl Chloroform. Wear goggles, a lab coat and gloves when dealing with phenol and chloroform. Chloroform Contributed by users
Let the samples incubate for 10 min at room temperature in a Thermomixer. Vortex from time to time. 00:10:00

Centrifuge for 15 min at 14.000 g, 4 ˚C. Transfer the upper aqueous phase ( contains RNA ) to a fresh reaction tube and add the same volume (450 µL) of Aqua- P/C/I (Phenol/Chloroform/Isoamylalcohol). 00:15:00 Wear goggles, a lab coat and gloves when dealing with phenol and chloroform.

Thoroughly mix by vortexing. Centrifuge for 15 min at high speed. Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a 1.5 mL reaction tube. 00:15:00 Wear goggles, a lab coat and gloves when dealing with phenol and chloroform.
Add 1 volume of isopropanol. Isopropanol Contributed by users
Mix and incubate for at least 30 min at -20 ˚C. (Can be left overnight.) 00:30:00
Wash with 75% chilled ethanol. Avoid resuspending the pellet. Ethyl alcohol, Pure 200 proof, for molecular biology Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #E7023
Repeat washing step with 75% chilled ethanol. Remove excess of EtOH by using a pipet.
Air dry pellet at RT. Do not overdry! 00:10:00
Resuspend the pellet with 30 µL volume of ddH 2 O. Usually 40 µL DECP-treated H 2 O
Fill 50 mL Falcon tube with ice. Fill with bacterial liquid culture, up to a volume of ~45 mL.
Discard supernatant. Resuspend cell pellet in residual water (~1 mL). Transfer to 2 mL 'safe lock' tube.
